Caption: A schematic of a lithium-ion (Li-ion) battery consisting of a graphite anode and LiCoO2 (LCO) cathode. During discharge, Li+ ions migrate through the electrolyte from the anode to the cathode, whilst corresponding electrons pass through the external circuit. Highlighted within this figure are the coatings and interfaces discussed in this review – the solid-electrolyte interphase (SEI), cathode-electrolyte interphase (CEI), and artificial coatings.

Caption: Schematic illustrations depicting DNP polarisation transfer in from (a) an external TEKPol radical (exogenous DNP) and (b) Fe3+ dopants within a TiO2 substrate. (c) 7Li NMR spectra of a model TiO2 substrate with an artificial LixSiyOz coating with (red) and without (grey) DNP enhancement. Figure adapted with permission from Ref. [31]: Haber et al. J. Am. (a) INADEQUATE spectrum recorded for an SEI formed on an Si-based anode. (b) and (c) correspond to the regions of interest highlighted in (a) (green and purple respectively). Asterisks denote spinning sidebands. Figure adapted with permission from Ref. [30]: Michan et al. J. Am. Chem. Soc. 138, 7918–7931. Copyright 2016 American Chemical Society.
